behavior_summary: U8P_TABLWARE::look is the object's examine/U8P_TABLWARE::look handler. When the player inspects the object it emits a short descriptive text "tableware " and then the process suspends/ends.
There are no menus, branching, global flag checks/sets, or external calls in the provided script — the response is static and unconditional.
The code appears to be purely presentational: provide a short description to the player on U8P_TABLWARE::look and then stop.
notable_control_flow: Single unconditional path: on entry to U8P_TABLWARE::look emit one Bark ('tableware ') and then suspend the process.
No conditional branches, no menu/Ask nodes, and no internal or external function calls present in the parsed nodes.

Is the trailing 'suspend' node intended to fully terminate the U8P_TABLWARE::look process or only to yield; how the engine treats this suspend in the object's lifecycle is inferred but not explicit.
Are there other related processes or higher-level scripts that reference U8P_TABLWARE instances (none appear in this excerpt)?
